I want, we are, I, we are always trying
to provide opportunities to engage with our Maori.
Um, right now they're going through their own issues
with the Maori land court in terms of their recognition
of their trusteeships.
Um, so we will always,
and we always do, we've repeatedly, um, tried to hold
down there like we have, we have to hold our here, we have
to hold this hui here because we got denied
the booking at our marai.
We've gotta move on. We've gotta keep going forward.
We've gotta keep the, you know, doing what we need to do.
Um, the door's always open. It's my marae.
I'm always gonna go home. I'm always gonna work with my au
my au who I grew up with on this ua.
Um, so
absolutely, that's the dream,
Right? Um,
I'm actually deciding we're gonna have a,
a short break now, but
before we do go for that break, I want
to pass, uh, a few comments.
Um, we, we all know
that treaty settlement processes, uh,
are nowhere near perfect.
It is full of compromise, uh, through the processes.
We know it, you know it itself.
Um, and there's these financial red reduces,
cultural redress, apologies, all that sort of stuff
that you expect in a, in, in a, in a settlement process.
But to my mind, uh, there will be
limited success moving forward
without the most important component.
And that is the, uh, the people who are taking it forward.
And it's all of you.
And, uh, clearly, uh, there's, uh,
some communication that needs to be had.
The reason why I wanna hold a break right now is
'cause my experiences, as soon as we finish these hearings,
everybody goes their own ways
and you lose your opportunity to have the cup of tea.
You know what I mean? This cup of tea
in the next 20 minutes could be the most important one
for your future generations, which is
what this should be aimed at.
I ask you to consider that
and see what you can do by potentially the failings
of the communications of the past.
And look at this as a settlement is, is look at this
as a potential new beginning, A new start for you
as a people, all of you, about where you go to,
you got challenges and that don't talk about issues,
talk about solutions together
because it's one thing that's probably outta your control.
It's very likely we're gonna be
passing a settlement bill here.
It is the process that we're gonna be unprecedented.
If we don't do that, it's logical, it will happen.
It's not up to us of what that provides
as an opportunity for you.
It is all of you in the room.
I encourage you to, to go up
to one another and start again.
Have a new beginning, say hello again and,
and begin a new conversation.
Because with brick walls between you, with, with barriers
between you, it is not good
for your future generations around you today.
Think about the future. Don't think about the past, past.
Don't think about what's being, think about what you can be
as a people going forward.
I'm sorry to sound like a bit of a lecturer here,
but I see this as a great opportunity for you.
Great opportunity. Use this as the beginning
of a new beginning for your coordinate,
or that potentially did fail in the
past for whatever reason.
We're not gonna cure that today,
but what we can cure today is how you actually, hopefully,
respectfully, constructively, actually have a cup of tea
and start a new conversation.
I ask you all that. That's what I say. I ask you all that.
So with that, let's, let's have a, at least have a break,
have a cup of tea and we'll reconvene, uh,
to maybe it's say, uh, 10, uh, 1135.
Does that sound about right?
I what time?
You gimme a suggestion.
1115 ka Okay.
Yeah.
